description: Static configuration-contract scenarios for configure-gtm. These are deterministic specification fixtures, not model-output or runtime tests.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to produce a read-only GA4 purchase configuration from an approved tracking-plan row and representative zero-, one-, and multi-item payloads. A compatible Google tag exists. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Config
expected_actions.justification: Compatible Google tag already exists.

expected_actions.name: CE - purchase
expected_actions.justification: Approved vendor-neutral source event.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - purchase
expected_actions.justification: Approved GA4 ecommerce requirement.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to produce a read-only Meta Purchase specification from a human media brief. A partner integration already initializes the Pixel, the approved purchase event contains GA4-shaped items, and current Meta browser documentation requires a contents transformation. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Existing Meta partner integration
expected_actions.justification: Approved evidence confirms compatible initialization.

expected_actions.name: CJS - Meta - contents
expected_actions.justification: Official Meta array shape differs from the approved source shape.

expected_actions.name: Meta - Event - Purchase
expected_actions.justification: Human media brief requests the official browser event.

external_dependencies: Meta platform optimization or Custom Conversion state is recorded separately.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to specify strict/basic Meta gating where the approved CMP contract requires both advertising-purpose grant and the exact Meta vendor grant. Each state is exposed through a separate documented native GTM-compatible variable. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Block - CMP - advertising purpose denied
expected_actions.justification: Independent required purpose grant needs OR-denial behavior.

expected_actions.name: Block - CMP - Meta denied
expected_actions.justification: Independent required vendor grant needs OR-denial behavior.

external_dependencies: CMP must continue to supply the approved documented states.
expected_invariants: both blocks attach to every Meta consumer
forbidden_invariants: one trigger with mutually exclusive AND denials

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to design GA4 basic blocking and Google Ads advanced Consent Mode on one currently shared Google tag. No officially supported approved separation has been supplied. No runtime access is available.
external_dependencies: Analyst must approve a current officially supported execution-unit separation or compatible policy.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm for a generate_lead event where the human requirement names the business action but supplies no success moment, dataLayer event, field types, or representative payload. No runtime access is available.
external_dependencies: Approved source event, success timing, field contract, and representative payload are required.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm for an approved GA4 sign_up event with a complete source contract and container export, but no MCP, API, or authorized UI mutation path. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: CE - sign_up
expected_actions.justification: Approved vendor-neutral source event.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - sign_up
expected_actions.justification: Approved analytics requirement and official event contract.

external_dependencies: An authorized mutation path is required to apply the specification.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm in an authorized dedicated workspace. A constant and DLV save successfully, then the approved community-template tag write fails. No runtime access is available.
scenarios.recovery_boundary: Stop the failed tag and all dependent writes; preserve unrelated and successfully saved current-operation objects pending an authorized recovery decision.

expected_actions.name: CST - vendor pixel_id
expected_actions.justification: Approved stable destination identity.

expected_actions.name: DLV - event.value
expected_actions.justification: Approved source field.

external_dependencies: Resolve the template write failure before dependent mutation.
expected_invariants: exact saved IDs and fingerprints recorded

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm after a prior authorized implementation. Every saved object and reference matches the configuration contract and current fingerprints are supplied. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Existing approved implementation
expected_actions.justification: Every semantic dimension and saved field matches.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to specify an approved GA4 custom event with a custom event parameter intended for reporting and a requested key event. GTM mutation is not authorized. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - custom_event
expected_actions.justification: Approved analytics event requirement.

external_dependencies: Register the appropriate GA4 custom definition.
Designate the event as a key event outside the GTM authorization.
expected_invariants: GTM collection separated from GA4 administration

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm for an unlisted browser media vendor with an approved media brief, complete source contract, current official browser event and consent documentation, and an inspected template. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Official vendor browser implementation
expected_actions.justification: Generic media mapping contract is fully established by current official evidence.

external_dependencies: Platform-side conversion or optimization setup is recorded separately.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to produce a read-only GA4 specification from an approved tracking plan that explicitly selects the technically valid custom event account_created. Current official documentation identifies sign_up as a potentially more appropriate recommended event. The approved source contract is complete. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: CE - account_created
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved source event.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - account_created
expected_actions.justification: Exact technically valid approved analytics event.

external_dependencies: The tracking-plan owner may separately approve a future measurement-plan revision.
forbidden_invariants: tracking plan optimized by configuration skill

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm for an approved analytics row whose requested manual event is established by current official documentation as reserved and unsupported for the requested use. No amended analytics decision exists. No runtime access is available.
external_dependencies: The tracking-plan owner must supply an amended approved analytics decision.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to produce a read-only GA4 specification from an approved event containing exactly one approved parameter. Current official documentation identifies another compatible field as recommended but not required. No runtime access is available.

discrepancies.default_action: Keep the approved field set unchanged.

expected_actions.name: GA4 - Event - approved_custom_event
expected_actions.justification: Exact approved event and parameter set.

external_dependencies: Any parameter addition requires a new explicit analytics decision.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to produce a read-only specification from an approved workbook scope containing one visible event sheet, one explicitly approved hidden event sheet, one reference dictionary, and one explicitly excluded legacy sheet. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Two approved event implementations
expected_actions.justification: The explicit source-scope manifest includes both event sheets.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to produce a read-only event specification where the approved source value is directly available through a DLV, but the existing container commonly uses broad Custom JavaScript helpers for similar values. The helper has no required consumer relationship. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: DLV - event.plan_type
expected_actions.justification: The best-practice direct mapping is selected before local reuse.

expected_actions.name: Existing broad Custom JavaScript helper
expected_actions.justification: It is nonconformant and outside cleanup scope.

external_dependencies: Existing unrelated helper debt is reported for any separate cleanup review.
forbidden_invariants: container prevalence treated as best practice

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm for an approved event when a confirmed existing automatic implementation would duplicate the best-practice manual event. Updating or disabling the existing implementation is not authorized. No runtime access is available.
external_dependencies: The analyst must authorize reconciliation of the conflicting existing implementation.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm for a read-only specification through an adapter whose current schema exposes product-specific action names, a maximum page size of twenty for one object family, cursor pagination, and batch readback limits. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Current-run discovered schema
expected_actions.justification: Exact actions, limits, cursors, and returned fields are established before mutation.

external_dependencies: An authorized mutation path is required to apply the specification.

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm in an authorized dedicated workspace containing two confirmed pre-existing changes. The current operation updates one approved tag, saved readback passes, and the identical rerun is a no-op. No runtime access is available.

expected_actions.name: Approved analytics event
expected_actions.justification: The current operation changes only the approved tag.

external_dependencies: Publication remains outside this skill.
expected_invariants: final total not attributed to current run

scenarios.request: Use configure-gtm to add a server-side GTM route, browser/server event IDs, and CAPI deduplication to an otherwise approved client-side analytics and media design. No runtime access is available.
external_dependencies: A future authorized server-side and deduplication capability must own this requirement.
